title: Module: reading a trial registry record / protocol
context: Patient advocates on trial steering committees and ethics boards frequently need to read ClinicalTrials.gov records and protocols. This module teaches them how a registry record is structured, what each field means, and what to look for — explicitly scoped to understanding structure, not to recommending or selecting trials. It extends the M1 curriculum and is itself patient-facing high-risk content.
objective: Author an original, plain-language educational module on how to read a clinical-trial registry record (e.g. ClinicalTrials.gov entry) and a protocol document — teaching structure and field meaning, not trial selection or recommendation.
acceptanceCriteria: Covers key fields of a ClinicalTrials.gov record: NCT number, trial phase, status, sponsor, primary/secondary endpoints, eligibility criteria, arms and interventions, primary completion date, and results section when available.
Explains what each field means for appraisal and advocacy (e.g. eligibility criteria determine who a result applies to; primary endpoint determines what the trial is powered to show).

Explicitly scoped: teaches how to understand a registry record's structure; does not recommend or assess whether a specific trial is appropriate for any individual — that is explicitly out of scope and flagged as such.
Carries a persistent 'education only — not medical advice' notice and passes the advice-language checklist.
Every factual claim cited to a sources.yaml entry with compatible allowedUse; passes the licence + provenance build gate and CI.
Signed off by a credentialed oncologist (recorded in the review ledger): clinical framing is correct and nothing reads as trial-selection guidance.
Signed off by an experienced patient advocate (recorded in the review ledger): content is accessible and does not stray into advice.
Signed off by a methodology reviewer: registry record interpretation is accurate.
resources: PLAN.md (§ Non-goals — not a trial finder; § Goals — Goal 1)
output: A plain-language educational module (content/modules/reading-registry-records.md) committed to the repo, teaching registry record structure and field meaning, provenance-cited, CI-green, and carrying dual high-tier sign-off plus methodology review.
